title: arthropodium glaucum
intro: Arthropodium glaucum, also known as milkmaids or orchid flax, is a perennial plant native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its striking blue-green foliage and delicate white flowers. Traditionally, the roots of Arthropodium glaucum have been used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Arthropodium glaucum is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is largely anecdotal and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
